_readme: Content OS · M2 material source catalog — DATA ONLY. One object per source; add a source = add one object + redeploy, no code change. Full field reference, Bright Data mechanics and the collect-vs-discover / external_id traps are documented in readme.md — keep prose there, not here. NEVER put API keys in this file: 'auth_ref' names a Supabase secret (Bright Data uses BRIGHTDATA_API_KEY automatically). Knows nothing about pages — page wiring lives in page_material_sources / page_reference_sources / page_article_sources.

sources.name: tmdb_movie_list
sources.notes: RAZ-23 shadow of Workflow A. {list} = now_playing|upcoming|top_rated (same shape). Workflow A pulls zh-TW/TW + en-US/US = two page rows per list.

sources.name: tmdb_trending
sources.notes: RAZ-23 shadow of Workflow A. region optional — leave blank in the en-US page row.

sources.name: tmdb_search
sources.notes: Keyword search (RAZ-26 + RAZ-37). Matches TITLES only — no plot/keyword search.

sources.name: tmdb_watch_providers
sources.notes: SCAFFOLD (disabled, RAZ-24). Per-movie ({movie_id}) and returns results keyed by country, not an array — needs movie-driven routing, not a standalone list.

sources.name: article_rss
sources.notes: SCAFFOLD (disabled, RAZ-25). {feed_url} filled per page_article_sources row; scrape-mode articles arrive via ingest.

sources.name: reference_harvest
sources.notes: GENERIC ingest fallback. NOT used by the RAZ-36 harvest — that ingests under each platform's own bd_* entry so the platform-verified field_map (incl. composite engagement) applies; this entry's bare 'engagement' path would need n8n to pre-shape, i.e. per-platform knowledge in the orchestrator. tier=inspiration = reference ONLY, never verbatim (contract guardrail).

sources.name: bd_perplexity
sources.notes: AI-search. PROVEN LIVE 2026-07-16, ~78s — slowest of the five.

sources.name: bd_chatgpt
sources.notes: AI-search. Proven live 2026-07-16, ~69s. bd_params.web_search=true — the scraper's own default is false.

sources.name: bd_gemini
sources.notes: AI-search. Proven live 2026-07-16, ~30s. No web_search control.

sources.name: bd_google_ai
sources.notes: AI-search. Proven live 2026-07-16, ~19s — FASTEST of the five, prefer as default. Optional 'hl' input sets answer language (add via bd_params).

sources.name: bd_copilot
sources.notes: AI-search. Proven live 2026-07-16, ~54s.

sources.name: bd_facebook
sources.notes: Social. The ONLY collect-mode profile->posts scraper — hence no bd_discover_by (see readme). field_map CORRECTED against real harvests 2026-07-16 (10 posts, then 30 incl. 2 Reels): image_url=attachments.0.url — the ONLY image path that works for every post type (post_image is null on Reels; attachments.0.thumbnail_url is null on Photos; attachments.0.url is an scontent image for both, and the first frame for a carousel). post_external_image is the LINK-PREVIEW image, null on normal image posts. title=content (page_name is the page, identical on every post). A FB post has no title — title and summary are both the caption by design. kind=post_type so Reels/Videos are distinguishable from photo posts. media=attachments carries the whole array, so carousels keep every image and videos keep attachments[].video_url + thumbnail_url. play_count is deliberately NOT in engagement: engagement is SUMMED for best_performing ranking, and plays dwarf likes, so a Reel would win permanently (views != likes — same reason as the cross-source rule). page_logo/header_image/avatar_image_url are all page-level, never per-post.

sources.name: bd_instagram
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=url PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (10 posts) — it is this group's ONLY discover mode. field_map CORRECTED against real records: summary was 'caption' and image_url was 'display_url' — NEITHER key exists (both 0/10); the caption is 'description' and the image is 'thumbnail'. title was user_posted (the author) -> now description. kind=content_type. No shares metric on IG. TWO CAVEATS: (1) discover_by=url returns posts APPEARING ON the profile incl. collabs/paid partnerships — for interstellarmovie only 2/10 were actually authored by it (rest were paramountpics etc. with coauthor_producers=interstellarmovie). Instagram collabs legitimately appear on both grids, but 'reference page' means something looser here than on Facebook. (2) All 10 came back content_type=Reel; discovery_input exposes a post_type param we do not set — likely why no photo posts. Set it via bd_params if photo posts are wanted.

sources.name: bd_x
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=profile_url PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (10 tweets). field_map CORRECTED against real records: title was user_posted = the handle, identical on every tweet -> now description (the tweet text; X has no title). There is NO 'engagement' object — the counts are flat (likes/replies/reposts/quotes/views/bookmarks). views + bookmarks are deliberately EXCLUDED: engagement is summed for best_performing and views dwarf likes (same reason as Facebook play_count). image_url=photos.0 fills 2/10 — real, most tweets are text-only. hashtags path exists but was 0/10 on this account: UNVERIFIED.

sources.name: bd_tiktok
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=profile_url PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (10 posts, netflix). field_map CORRECTED against real records: title was profile_username (the account, 1-distinct) -> description. No 'engagement' object; flat counts -> {likes:digg_count, comments:comment_count, shares:num_share_count}. play_count + collect_count EXCLUDED from engagement (views dwarf likes — same rule as FB play_count). kind=post_type. media=video_url. topic_tags=hashtags. carousel_images exists (null here) — TikTok photo-carousels would populate it; UNVERIFIED, this account posts video only.

sources.name: bd_youtube
sources.notes: Social. RECONFIGURED 2026-07-16 per owner: dataset gd_lk538t2k2p1k3oos71 takes CHANNEL /about URLs natively (BD example: youtube.com/@MrBeast/about) — no discover params needed, so no bd_discover_by. ref_url MUST be the channel /about URL. The old config (gd_lk56epmy2i5g7lzu0k + discover_by=url) crawled unbounded and never finished. BD also offers keyword/hashtag video discovery on this family — separate entries if ever wanted. field_map UNVERIFIED for this dataset's schema — audit on first run.

sources.name: bd_linkedin
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=company_url PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (netflix). field_map CORRECTED against a real record: external_id was post_id — NO SUCH KEY (0/N, dedup-breaking); the id is 'id'. title was user_id (the account) -> post_text. No 'engagement' object; flat -> {likes:num_likes, comments:num_comments} (no share count on LinkedIn). kind=post_type, topic_tags=hashtags. image_url=video_thumbnail works for VIDEO posts; image-only posts populate 'images' (array) and leave video_thumbnail null — single-path limitation, UNVERIFIED for image posts.

sources.name: bd_reddit
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=subreddit_url; input schema (from BD validation echo) is url + sort_by + sort_by_time + keyword + start_date. sort_by='new' (lowercase) is REJECTED by validation; 'Hot' passes — probe-confirmed 2026-07-16. 'New' capitalized untested (account went inactive mid-probe); switch to it for true latest_n once confirmed. Without sort/limit the discover crawled unbounded and never finished the poll window. field_map unverified — audit on first run.

sources.name: bd_pinterest
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=profile_url PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (9 pins, imdb). field_map CORRECTED against real records: title was user_name (the account, 1-distinct) — Pinterest DOES have a real per-pin 'title'; summary=content, image=image_video_url (was 'description'/'image_url', neither exists). kind=post_type, topic_tags=hashtags. No shares on Pinterest; likes/comments_num are genuinely low/0 — real data, not a bad map.

sources.name: bd_quora
sources.notes: Social. NEEDS dataset_id — no screenshot yet. Errors defensively until pasted.

sources.name: bd_snapchat
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=profile PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (netflix, 28 records / 34 dead_page errors — spotlight scraping is lossy). field_map CORRECTED against real records: title AND summary were user_name/content — NEITHER KEY EXISTS, so every record was dropped for having no title (pulled=0). Caption is 'description'. published_at was date_posted -> upload_date (no date_posted key). kind='title' (Snapchat's generic post-type label, e.g. 'Spotlight Snap'). image=thumbnail_url, media=video_url, topic_tags=hashtags. Engagement flat {num_comments,num_shares}; no likes on Snapchat; num_views excluded (views rule).

sources.name: bd_vimeo
sources.notes: Social. RE-ENABLED 2026-07-16 (vimeo2.png): 'Discover by url' DOES take channel URLs, but its input schema is url + keyword + pages — the earlier 400 came from sending url alone. bd_params supplies keyword:'' + pages:1. Confirm which channel-URL form BD accepts on first run. field_map unverified — audit on first run.

sources.name: bd_threads
sources.notes: DISABLED + needs dataset_id — confirm Bright Data offers a Threads scraper before enabling.
